answer:
The output signal-to-noise ratio for FM is given by \((S_o/N_o) = 3\beta^2 (S_i/N_i)\), where \(S_i/N_i\) is 40 dB (or 10,000 in linear). Converting to dB: \(S_o/N_o \text{ (dB)} = 10\log_{10}(3\beta^2) + 40\).  
For \(\beta = 5\): \(10\log_{10}(3 \times 25) \approx 18.75\) dB, total ≈ 58.75 dB; \(\beta = 10\): \(10\log_{10}(300) \approx 24.77\) dB, total ≈ 64.77 dB; \(\beta = 105\): \(10\log_{10}(3 \times 11025) \approx 45.185\) dB, total ≈ 85.185 dB.  
These values match option A.

\boxed{A}
